source: Key2Vec- Automatic Ranked Keyphrase Extraction from Scientific Articles using Phrase Embeddings.pdf

concepts.finding:
In this paper, we present an un-
supervised technique (Key2Vec) that leverages
phrase embeddings for ranking keyphrases
extracted from scientiﬁc articles.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Speciﬁ-
cally, we propose an effective way of pro-
cessing text documents for training multi-word
phrase embeddings that are used for thematic
representation of scientiﬁc articles and rank-
ing of keyphrases extracted from them using
theme-weighted PageRank.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Due to extensive use
of phrase embeddings for representing the can-
didate keyphrases and ranking them, we name
our method as Key2Vec.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:

ing results in the process of keyphrase extraction
from scientiﬁc articles (Wang et al., 2015, 2014).
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
did not use domain-speciﬁc
word embeddings and had suggested that train-
ing them might lead to improvements.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
In this work, we represent candidate keyphrases
extracted from a scientiﬁc article by domain-
speciﬁc phrase embeddings and rank them using
a theme-weighted PageRank algorithm (Langville
and Meyer, 2004), such that the thematic weight
of a candidate keyphrase indicate how similar
it is to the themat
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
shows ranked keyphrases extracted using
Key2Vec from a sample research abstract.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
2
Methodology
Our methodology primarily uses three steps: can-
didate selection, candidate scoring, and candidate
ranking, similar to other popular frameworks of
ranked keyphrase extraction (Kim et al., 2013).
concepts.implication: Relevant for similarity-based retrieval and lookalike targeting

concepts.finding:
Tables 3 and 4
shows a comparison of Key2Vec with some of the
state-of-the-art systems giving best performances
on the Inspec and SemEval 2010 datasets, respec-
tively.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
4
Conclusion and Future Work
In this paper, we proposed a framework for auto-
matic extraction and ranking of keyphrases from
scientiﬁc articles.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
We showed an efﬁcient way of
training phrase embeddings, and showed its effec-
tiveness in constructing thematic representation of
scientiﬁc articles and assigning thematic weights
to candidate keyphrases.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Experimental evaluations conﬁrm
that our proposed technique of Key2Vec produces
state-of-the-art results on benchmark datasets.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
The task of ranked keyphrase extraction
from scientiﬁc articles is of great interest to sci-
entiﬁc publishers as it helps to recommend arti-
cles to readers, highlight missing citations to au-
thors, identify potential reviewers for submissions,
and analyze research trends over time (Augenstein
et
concepts.implication: Can inform intent modeling for recommendation systems

concepts.finding:
Due to its widespread use, keyphrase
extraction has received signiﬁcant attention from
researchers (Kim et al., 2010; Augenstein et al.,
2017).
concepts.implication: Architecture pattern applicable to sequence modeling for user behavior

concepts.finding:
Some of the major challenges are the var-
ied length of the documents to be processed, their
structural inconsistency and developing strategies
that can perform well in different domains (Hasan
and Ng, 2014).
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Methods for automatic keyphrase extraction are
mainly divided into two categories: supervised
and unsupervised.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Supervised methods approach
the problem as a binary classiﬁcation problem
(Hasan and Ng, 2014), whereas the unsupervised
methods are mostly based on TF-IDF, cluster-
ing, and graph-based ranking (Hasan and Ng,
2010; Mihalcea and Tarau, 2004).
concepts.implication: Applicable to user/item clustering for personalization

concepts.finding:
On the pres-
ence of domain-speciﬁc data, supervised methods
have shown better performance.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
The unsupervised
methods have the advantage of not requiring any
training data and can produce results in any do-
main.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation
